title: Cat Vomiting Yellow Bile: Causes, Urgency, and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Understand why your cat is vomiting yellow bile, the urgency level of their symptoms, and how to effectively triage their condition.
content_html:
TL;DR: While yellow bile vomit can be caused by an empty stomach, persistent vomiting, lethargy, or loss of appetite are signs of serious underlying health issues that require a veterinary consultation.

What is the yellow liquid my cat is vomiting?

When a cat vomits a yellow, frothy liquid, it is typically bile. Bile is a digestive fluid produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. It enters the small intestine to help break down food. If a cat vomits on an empty stomach, the irritation from these acidic fluids often results in the expulsion of this bright yellow or greenish liquid.

Is it an emergency if my cat is vomiting yellow bile?

  • Urgency Level: Medium. A one-off episode may be minor, but persistent vomiting should never be ignored.
  • Contact a vet if your cat vomits more than once in 24 hours.
  • Seek medical attention if your cat appears lethargic or refuses to eat.
  • Be aware that these symptoms can indicate serious issues like liver disease, kidney problems, or intestinal blockages.
  • Monitor your pet closely and seek help if their behavior changes.

Why should I take a photo of my cat's vomit for the veterinarian?

  • Allows the medical team to see the exact color, consistency, and volume.
  • Helps identify the presence of any hidden foreign objects, blood, or parasites.
  • Assists the triage team in determining how quickly your cat needs to be seen.
  • Helps the veterinarian determine which diagnostic tests might be necessary.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Vomiting yellow bile in cats can be indicative of extrahepatic biliary duct obstruction (EHBDO) or other hepatobiliary disorders. Bile stasis in obstructed ducts can lead to bilirubin deconjugation, reducing bilirubin solubility and favoring cholelith precipitation. Emergency surgery may be necessary in some cases, and careful exploration of all biliary structures is warranted during surgery to determine patency of the cystic and common bile ducts, as well as the viability of the gallbladder. Cholecystectomy is often the treatment of choice, although cholecystoenterostomy or choledochoenterostomy may be beneficial to circumvent a permanently occluded distal common bile duct.

Chapter: Gastroenterology, Hepatology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 479)

faqs.question: Is yellow bile different from a hairball?
faqs.answer: Yes. A hairball usually contains a dense mass of fur, whereas yellow bile is a clear or frothy liquid. However, a hairball stuck in the digestive tract can sometimes cause a cat to vomit bile.

faqs.question: Should I withhold food if my cat is vomiting bile?
faqs.answer: You should generally wait a few hours before offering food to see if the vomiting persists. If the cat seems hungry and alert, a small, bland meal may help, but always consult a professional if the vomiting happens again.

faqs.question: Can stress cause a cat to vomit bile?
faqs.answer: Yes, significant stress can lead to gastrointestinal upset in cats, which may result in vomiting bile due to increased stomach acid production.
